commit.message:
Clarify how live-lab proof is separated from the standards baseline (#29)

The replacement task plan and notes index now spell out the
milestone-2 versus milestone-3 boundary instead of leaving end-to-end
acceptance as a single Phase 9 placeholder. A new live-lab validation
lanes note ties the existing runbook, evidence bundle, and dashboard
surfaces into three concrete review lanes.

Constraint: YON-50 requires reviewable child lanes with explicit evidence expectations
Rejected: Add only a new note without touching the task plan or indexes | would leave the roadmap entrypoints ambiguous
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep milestone-2 claim-setting docs separate from milestone-3 live-lab proof and operator evidence docs
Tested: rg wording scan; git diff --check; npm run contracts:ran-replacement; mix test
Not-tested: GitHub PR checks

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: release v1.6.0 with richer schema extraction and field errors

Release focus

- release 1.6.0
- keep the release notes focused on the key changes
- commit with co-author
- push the gem and GitHub release after the commit

Key changes

- expand ActiveRecord schema extraction with except, on, required, and merge
- extract presence into allow_blank: false without overloading required semantics
- support field-level error and error_message overrides for validation and coercion failures
- keep merge keyed by raw field names and let merged required=false remove bang-style output names
- sync README and README_zh with the updated field option and schemas docs

Verification

- bundle exec rspec

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: expand ActiveRecord schema extraction controls

User requests

- schemas add except option
- check whether ActiveRecord validation presence and allow_blank are extracted
- our gem allows changing the semantics of required, so keep that in mind when mapping presence
- check how validations with if and unless are handled
- schemas add an on option so validate on can be extracted
- schemas add a required option that can be true, false, or an array
- when required is an array, only those fields are required and every other field is treated as non-required
- document that rule in the README
- commit with co-author

Implementation

- add schemas(except: ...) and keep it composable with only:
- extract presence into allow_blank: false and avoid overloading it into required semantics alone
- skip validators with if:/unless: during static schema extraction
- support schemas(on: ...) so on:/except_on: validators can be extracted for a requested validation context
- add schemas(required: true | false | [fields]) as an explicit requiredness override for exported fields
- update README and README_zh with the new schemas options and semantics
- expand ActiveRecord schema specs to cover filtering, contexts, required overrides, and validator extraction edge cases

Verification

- bundle exec rspec

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: refine blank coercion and required blank defaults

User requests

- Check whether allow_blank still errors on coercion failures such as Date with an empty string.
- allow_blank itself means the value is allowed and should not error.
- For empty values that cannot be converted, set them to nil.
- When a field allows blank, type coercion should not fail just because the input is an empty string.
- Add a setting for whether required fields allow_blank, default allow_blank=true.

Implementation

- Normalize blank-string coercion so meaningful blank values stay as-is while non-meaningful typed blanks become nil.
- Add config.required_allow_blank with field-level blank / allow_blank overrides.
- Update README and README_zh and cover the behavior with validator and ActiveRecord schema specs.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mcp-core): Expose get_sentry_resource by default (#852)

Promote get_sentry_resource to the stable tool surface and hide the
legacy issue and trace detail tools behind it.

This fixes the confusion from GH-850 where get_sentry_resource and
breadcrumbs were only reachable in experimental mode. The legacy detail
handlers still exist so get_sentry_resource can compose them internally,
but they are now marked internal-only and filtered out of server
registration, generated definitions, plugin allowedTools, and
use_sentry's embedded tool surface.

I also updated public guidance and plugin prompts to point to
get_sentry_resource instead of the legacy tool names, and regenerated
the derived definitions so the stable and experimental plugin manifests
stay in sync.

Fixes GH-850

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sentry-mcp
repository.description: An MCP server for interacting with Sentry via LLMs.

commit.message:
fix(junior): Lazy-init runtime for route builds (#125)

Avoid build-time runtime initialization in the Junior route handlers and
keep the example app covered in CI.

Next was evaluating the queue callback route during build, which pulled
in the production Slack runtime at module load. That forced
`SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET` and `REDIS_URL` to exist during `next build`,
even though those are runtime requirements.

This change moves production app assembly behind lazy getters so the
production Slack bot, runtime, and Redis-backed state adapter are only
created when a request needs them. The queue callback and webhook
handlers now rely on those getters, which keeps route-module import safe
during `next build`. It also restores the missing `cli/env` tsup entry
so the published `junior` bin can still run `snapshot create`.

The PR also keeps the example app covered in CI by running `pnpm exec
next build` from `apps/example`. That checks the representative Next
build path without introducing an example-only build wrapper or
requiring snapshot sandbox auth in CI.

I considered removing the runtime config checks entirely, but that
weakens real request-time validation. The problem was the lifecycle
boundary, not the checks themselves.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

- document the compact issue-row behavior in the FrankenTUI UI notes

  issue consumed two rendered rows
- compact rows improve scanability without taking space away from the
  timeline or detail panes

Tests:

commit.message:
fix(openhands): recreate sessions on LLM config drift

Summary:
- persist a hashed LLM config fingerprint in conversation manifests and
  compare it against the current workflow-resolved OpenHands request
  before reusing a conversation
- hard-recreate drifted conversations with DELETE plus same-ID POST,
  then recover context by appending Linear Codex workpad state to the
  first full prompt of the recreated fresh thread
- add Linear workpad comment lookup, CLI runtime wiring, fake-server
  delete coverage, and docs for the new recovery path

Rationale:
- same-ID POST preserves OpenHands history but does not update stored
  provider config, so credential rotation needs an explicit delete and
  recreate flow to avoid stale auth failures
- the manifest fingerprint avoids storing provider secrets in plaintext
  while still letting the runner detect model or env-value drift before
  attach and preserve issue-level continuity through the Linear workpad

Tests:
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands
- cargo test -p opensymphony-linear
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli
- cargo clippy -p opensymphony-openhands -p opensymphony-linear -p opensymphony-cli --tests -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(sandbox): Restore ambient Vercel auth resolution (#123)

Restore the sandbox auth behavior we had before the recent
sandbox/runtime refactors.

The sandbox credential helper started treating `VERCEL_TEAM_ID` and
`VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` as an incomplete explicit credential set, which made
Vercel prod fail before `@vercel/sandbox` could resolve ambient auth.
This change defers back to the SDK when running inside Vercel and keeps
explicit token validation for non-Vercel environments.

I also loaded `.env*` for local `pnpm exec junior ...` flows so `junior
snapshot create` and `junior check` match the existing repo wrapper
during local development. I considered removing the helper entirely, but
we still want the clearer failure for real partial token-based
configuration outside Vercel. Added focused unit coverage for both
paths.

---------

ref(evals): Extract evals into dedicated package (#120)

Extract all evals from `packages/junior/evals/` into a new private
`packages/junior-evals/` package to fix a dependency inversion where the
core package's eval suite depended on plugin packages
(`@sentry/junior-github`, `@sentry/junior-sentry`).

Evals are now organized by domain rather than lumped together:
- `evals/core/` — core runtime behavior (routing, lifecycle, passive
behavior, oauth, skill infra)
- `evals/github/` — GitHub plugin evals (issue create, default repo
setup, credential smoke)
- `evals/sentry/` — Sentry plugin evals (credential smoke)
- `evals/fixtures/` — eval-local test plugins and skills

The eval harness resolves `@sentry/junior` internals via vitest path
aliases (`@/` and `@junior-tests/`) rather than new package exports,
keeping the core package's public surface unchanged. The
`behavior-harness.test.ts` unit test moves to the evals package since it
tests the harness directly.

No behavior changes — all eval cases are preserved with the same
criteria and overrides.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

persistence, commands, and startup/help text.
* **Tests**
* End-to-end tests exercising REPL workflows, persistence rules,
commands, continuation detection, and linker/help behaviors.
* **Chores**
* Build configuration and project README updated to include the REPL
component.

merge: resync COE-281 with latest origin/main

Summary:
- resolve the third origin/main merge sweep across the COE-281 runtime,
  OpenHands session, test, and doc updates
- keep path-prefixed managed loopback normalization and MCP stdio
  forwarding while preserving mainline local_server.command,
  provider-env, and reuse-policy behavior
- retarget the CLI launcher-override regression test so it exercises a
  truly external unsupported transport instead of the now-supported
  path-prefixed loopback case

Rationale:
- keep PR #55 mergeable after new mainline drift without regressing the
  supported COE-281 transport and mcp_config behavior
- align the repository docs and runtime assertions with the merged
  runtime semantics for managed local OpenHands targets

commit.message:
fix(installer): default launcher to ~/.local/bin

Summary:
- change install-cli.sh to link the agent launcher into
  ~/.local/bin by default
- update uninstall-cli.sh and README to match the new
  default launcher location
- add a regression test that verifies the default HOME-based
  install and uninstall paths

Rationale:
- ~/.local/bin is already present on the local shell PATH,
  while ~/bin is not
- keeping the default launcher path on PATH removes an extra
  manual shell setup step after install

Tests:
- ./gradlew :agent-cli:test --tests agent.cli.InstallerScriptsTest
- AGENT_SKIP_BUILD=1 scripts/install-cli.sh --force
- command -v agent && agent version

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): restore release workflow task names

Summary:
- update the release workflow to call the renamed Gradle tasks
  for install verification and distribution archives
- switch release artifact paths, formula output, and artifact naming
  from `ccode` to `agent`

Rationale:
- release tagging was failing immediately because the workflow still
  invoked removed `ccode` task names
- keeping the workflow aligned with the renamed distribution artifacts
  avoids follow-on failures later in the release job

Tests:
- `./gradlew test verifyAgentInstallDist agentDistTar agentDistZip`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(git): ignore .agent workspace state

Summary:
- add a repo-wide ignore rule for `.agent/`
- keep the legacy `.crackedcode-agent/` ignore rule in place

Rationale:
- the default workspace state directory now lives under `.agent/`
- ignoring it prevents session state and config files from showing up
  as untracked files or being committed by accident

Tests:
- git check-ignore -v .agent .agent/config.properties

commit.message:
fix(openhands): configure coding-agent filesystem tools

Summary:
- add explicit OpenHands agent tool configuration to workflow resolution
  and conversation-create payloads
- default coding agents to TerminalTool and FileEditorTool while
  preserving optional include_default_tools overrides
- persist tool selection in the launch profile, add regression tests,
  and update the OpenHands integration docs

Rationale:
- the previous request contract only serialized agent kind and llm,
  leaving OpenHands on its FinishTool/ThinkTool defaults
- resumed conversations need the same launch profile fields as fresh
  conversations or the runtime recreates a tool-less agent on rehydrate
- partial workflow agent overrides should stay usable without forcing
  callers to restate the default llm model

Tests:
- cargo test -p opensymphony-workflow
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands
- cargo clippy -p opensymphony-workflow -p opensymphony-openhands --all-targets -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
perf(translator): reduce script cache fingerprint churn

- memoize request-wide translation script cache fingerprints
- memoize visible history prefix fingerprints for repeated snapshot reads
- keep runtime fingerprint once per generated script cache key
- add regression tests for fingerprint reuse and key compression

feat(BUB-140): emit agentic derived OTLP metrics (#110)

Summary:
- emit agentic context, turn, cache, and tool metrics from
  compileOtlpMetrics
- support backend-folded context attrs used by CLI export
- add regression tests and field-spec coverage for the new metrics

Rationale:
- expose derived context metrics as OTLP time series without changing
  the normalize pipeline
- keep export behavior aligned across raw canonical events and
  backend-mapped turn attrs

  packages/otlp-core/test/otlp.metrics.test.mjs

  test/unit/otlp.spec.ts test/bdd/replay.integration.spec.ts
